cataloging_subject.label: Sammlung altenglischer Legenden
cataloging_subject.scope_note: The complete eighteen-component scholarly anthology compiled and edited by Carl Horstmann and published in 1878: seven components in the Vernon collection and eleven Einzellegenden, including the prose Adam-and-Eve appendix. It is not one medieval work, one manuscript, the 1881 second series, or twenty-two independent works. Four components serialize two witness-version streams; witnesses, transcripts, print intermediaries, facsimiles, the U-M encoding, and the repository XML remain separate lineage layers.

catalog_summary.title: Sammlung altenglischer Legenden
catalog_summary.author: Anonymous Middle English authors and translators; William Paris (Cristine)

subject_statements.label: Creation, cosmology, and the natural world
Christian anti-Judaism, antisemitism, and anti-Jewish violence
notes: Records hostile Christian representation within the text rather than endorsing it.

scope.description: A broad anthology-level subject, with different emphases in individual components

summaries.value: Carl Horstmann's 1878 anthology presents eighteen Middle English religious and legendary components. Part I transcribes a seven-item Vernon-Manuscript sequence related to the Legenda aurea; Part II prints individual saints' lives, biblical and apocryphal narratives, a Christian-Jewish disputation, the romance of Roberd of Cisyle, and a prose Adam-and-Eve appendix. Four components preserve two serialized witness versions, while German editorial framing and Latin comparisons, rubrics, verses, and prayers remain part of the scholarly presentation.

Part I: Paula; Ambrosius; the maiden of Antioch; Theodora; Bernard; Augustine; Savinian and Savina. Part II: Kindheit Jesu; Canticum de Creatione; Magdalena; Marina; Eufrosyne; Cristine; Dorothe; Erasmus; A disputisoun bytwene a cristenemon and a Jew; Roberd of Cisyle; appendix on Adam and Eve.

The record counts eighteen editorial components once. Kindheit Jesu, Canticum de Creatione, Magdalena, and Erasmus each own one item/form count but no line metrics; their eight zero-item version children partition those components' 348 verse groups and 5,070 lines. Together with the seven single-stream Part II components, they produce the Part II total of 480 groups and 7,617 lines. The whole encoding serializes twenty-two streams but does not contain twenty-two independent works.

extent.description: Eighteen count-bearing editorial components: seven in Part I and eleven in Part II. Four components have two serialized version children, producing twenty-two text streams and twenty-eight modeled part records when the two zero-count containers and eight version children are included. XML metrics are 670 LG elements and 13,275 L elements; Part I contributes 190/5,658 and Part II 480/7,617.

content_parts.label: Part I: Vernon collection
content_parts.summary: A seven-component sequence transcribed from the Vernon manuscript and compared with corresponding Legenda aurea chapters.

content_parts.label: S. Paula
content_parts.summary: A metrical legendary account of Saint Paula within the Vernon sequence.
notes: Three prose P elements are German editorial argument, not prose-form content of the item.

content_parts.label: S. Ambrosius
content_parts.summary: A metrical life of Saint Ambrose, including the nested Theodosius episode.

content_parts.label: De quadam virgine in Antiochia
content_parts.summary: The legend of a maiden of Antioch followed by the Sithia and Climonen continuation.

content_parts.label: De S. Theodora
content_parts.summary: A metrical legend of Saint Theodora.

content_parts.label: S. Bernard
content_parts.summary: A metrical life of Saint Bernard.

content_parts.label: S. Augustin
content_parts.summary: A metrical life of Saint Augustine.

content_parts.label: Savinian and Savina
content_parts.summary: A metrical legend of Savinian and Savina.

content_parts.label: Part II: Einzellegenden
content_parts.summary: Eleven individual legendary, biblical, debate, romance, and appendix components.

content_parts.label: Kindheit Jesu
content_parts.summary: Two serialized Middle English versions of narratives about Christ's childhood.
notes: One item/form count is owned here; the two zero-item children alone own the 137 groups and 1,538 lines.

content_parts.label: Kindheit Jesu: Harley MS 3954 version
content_parts.summary: The first serialized witness version.

content_parts.label: Kindheit Jesu: Harley MS 2399 version
content_parts.summary: The second serialized witness version.

content_parts.label: Canticum de Creatione
content_parts.summary: Two serialized creation narratives: the Trinity version and a composite Auchinleck stream surviving in Edinburgh and NLS fragments.
notes: One item/form count is owned here; the two zero-item children alone own the 202 groups and 1,983 lines.

content_parts.label: Canticum: Trinity College Oxford MS 57 version
content_parts.summary: The Trinity version, whose manuscript colophon dates the composition to 1375.
notes: Horstmann's fol. 156 citation is retained in lineage as historical foliation; the current catalog starts the item at fol. 157v.

content_parts.label: Canticum: composite Auchinleck version
content_parts.summary: One continuous version reconstructed from the detached EUL fragment for lines 1–352 and the NLS fragment for lines 353–end.
notes: The Horstmann stream reaches EUL MS 218 through Laing's print and reaches the NLS fragment through Lucy Toulmin Smith's transcript; it is not evidence of direct Horstmann autopsy of both carriers.

content_parts.label: Magdalena
content_parts.summary: Two serialized Middle English versions of the life of Mary Magdalene.
notes: One item/form count is owned here; the two zero-item children alone own the 3 groups and 1,325 lines. The prose P is an editorial loss note, not prose-form target content.

content_parts.label: Magdalena: Laud Misc. 108 version
content_parts.summary: The first serialized version, directly transcribed from Laud Misc. 108.

content_parts.label: Magdalena: Auchinleck version
content_parts.summary: The second serialized version, mediated through Lucy Toulmin Smith's transcript with Kölbing's transcript consulted.

content_parts.label: Marina
content_parts.summary: A metrical legend of Saint Marina.

content_parts.label: Eufrosyne
content_parts.summary: A metrical legend of Saint Euphrosyne.

content_parts.label: Cristine
content_parts.summary: William Paris's metrical life of Saint Christine.
notes: The current catalog gives the f. 2 start; the f. 4v endpoint is inferred from Dorothe beginning at f. 5r.

content_parts.label: Dorothe
content_parts.summary: A metrical legend of Saint Dorothy with Latin verses and two prose prayers; the final English lines are supplied from Arundel 168 after a Harley leaf loss.

content_parts.label: Erasmus
content_parts.summary: Two serialized versions of the Saint Erasmus legend; the Bedford stream continues from verse into a prose/list and prayer context.
notes: One item/form count is owned here; the two zero-item children alone own the 6 groups and 224 lines.

content_parts.label: Erasmus: Harley MS 2382 version
content_parts.summary: The first serialized Erasmus version.

content_parts.label: Erasmus: Bedford manuscript version
content_parts.summary: The second serialized Erasmus version: fifty verse lines followed by list and prayer material.

content_parts.label: A disputisoun bytwene a cristenemon and a Jew
content_parts.summary: A hostile Christian-Jewish disputation structured around Sir Walter's victory over a Jewish opponent.

content_parts.label: Roberd of Cisyle
content_parts.summary: An eclectic edition of the Middle English romance in which a proud king is displaced, humbled, and restored.
notes: Harley 1701, Harley 525, and CUL Ff.2.38 are collated witnesses. The unavailable CUL Ii.4.9 copy is not a source relation for this 1878 edition.

content_parts.label: Beilage zur Creatio mundi: þe lyff of Adam and Eue
content_parts.summary: A prose appendix recounting the creation and the life and sufferings of Adam and Eve.
notes: The XML contains fifteen content P elements. Horstmann received the text through a Furnivall working transcript rather than claiming direct autopsy of Vernon at this locus.

evidence.summary: Supplies the electronic title, U-M identifier, source-edition statement, languages in the retained text, editorial production note, component headings, content, and XML structure.

evidence.citation: XPath and semantic form audit of the pinned ajd8171 XML against Horstmann's printed pages.
evidence.locator: BODY DIV1/DIV2/DIV3, LG, L, P, and PB; Horstmann printed pp. 1–227
evidence.summary: Confirms eighteen count-bearing editorial components, four dual-version components and twenty-two streams, 670 LG elements, 13,275 L elements, and the 15 verse/2 mixed/1 prose form allocation. It distinguishes editorial prose from content prose and verifies every part metric.

evidence.citation: Linked ajd8171 lineage evidence for Horstmann's 1878 edition.
evidence.summary: Supplies the title-page, imprint, selection boundary, editorial responsibility, printed pagination, and source-description evidence.

evidence.citation: Linked ajd8171 lineage evidence for Horstmann's witness and intermediary mappings.
evidence.summary: Supplies current shelfmarks, direct and mediated editorial routes, exact folio endpoints, version boundaries, and facsimile mappings.

open_questions.question: What are the secure individual composition dates, dialect regions, and authorships of all eighteen medieval components?
next_steps: Add only component-scoped claims from current specialist scholarship; do not project witness or edition dates onto the whole selection.

open_questions.question: Can direct Arundel MS 168 images or a component catalog explicitly confirm Cristine's inferred endpoint at fol. 4v?
next_steps: Request or inspect ff. 2r–5r and retain f. 4v as an inference until the physical boundary is visible.

open_questions.question: What exact reading-level partition did Horstmann take from Vernon and Trinity MS 57 in the eclectic Roberd of Cisyle text?
next_steps: Collate every edition line against both witnesses before replacing the current explicitly unresolved dual-base scope with line-level source ranges.

notes: The two part containers, eight version children, and eighteen component parents are explicit accounting layers; only the component parents contribute item/form counts.
The XML's dedication and table-of-contents DIV1 milestones are representation-only and do not become content parts.
The 1881 second series is outside this cataloging boundary.
The raw header language code ger is retained as source history but does not describe the Middle English component texts adequately.
